titleStmt.title: SII 9.588: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 588. (A.R. No. 89 of 1912.) IN THE SAME PLACE.

summary.p: This is of the same date and reign as No. 587. It records that Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇayya purchased from certain Brāhmans (named) half of the village Sadāśivapura, surnamed Dēvarāyapura for varāhas 108 and made a gift of it for the service of the god Vīrabhadra of Lēpākshi. The village is stated to have been granted to Dēvara-Sōmayāji by king Prauḍha-Dēva-Maharāya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Etadgrāmasa�mopasthasadāśivapurāhvayaṁ . grāmaṁ cāsyaiva dēvasya kṣētrama
tra vilikhyatē . svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakava
ruṣaṁgaḷu 1460 neya viḷaṁbisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa ba 10 sō dallū
bēlūra cāvaḍage saluva deppaṟu sthaḷadalli I prauḍhadēvamahārāyariṁdalū I dēva
resōmayājigaḷige pratigrahalabhdavāda dēvarāyapuraveṁba pratināma
vuḷaṁthā sadāśivapura grāmadalli I koṁḍudīkṣitariṁda caturthāṁśavanu
sōmaṇasōmayājigaḷiṁda caturthāṁśavanū kūḍi I Ardhagrāmavanu I virapaṁ
ṇayyanavaru krayamūlya dravya ghaṭṭivaraha ga 108 Akṣāradallū nūrayeṁṭu varahakke
krayavanū tekoṁḍu �yo vīrēśvaradēvara Amṛtapaḍi naivēdya muṁtāda vaibhavaṁga
ḷige dhāreneradu koṭṭa sadāśivapura Ardhagrāmada śāsanada prati .
Digital edition of SII 9.588 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
